context.title: Circulatory system
context.text: The circulatory system is an organ system that permits blood to circulate and transport nutrients.

question: What is the name of the philosophical movement that emphasizes individual existence, freedom and choice?

context.title: Existentialism
context.text: Existentialism is a form of philosophical inquiry that explores the nature of the human condition.

context.title: Jean-Paul Sartre
context.text: Jean-Paul Sartre was a French philosopher, playwright, novelist, and political activist who was a key figure in existentialism.

question: Which historical region, located between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers, is often called the 'Cradle of Civilization'?

context.title: Mesopotamia
context.text: Mesopotamia is a historical region of Western Asia situated within the Tigris–Euphrates river system.

context.title: Fertile Crescent
context.text: The Fertile Crescent is a crescent-shaped region in the Middle East.

question: What is the name of the particle that carries the strong nuclear force in quantum chromodynamics?

context.title: Gluon
context.text: A gluon is an elementary particle that acts as the exchange particle for the strong force between quarks.

context.title: Strong interaction
context.text: The strong interaction is the mechanism responsible for the strong nuclear force.

question: Who was the last pharaoh of ancient Egypt and was a member of the Ptolemaic dynasty?

context.title: Cleopatra
context.text: Cleopatra VII Philopator was the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt.

context.title: Ptolemaic dynasty
context.text: The Ptolemaic dynasty was a Macedonian Greek royal family which ruled the Ptolemaic Kingdom in Egypt during the Hellenistic period.

question: What is the name of the theorem in geometry that states that the sum of the squares of the lengths of the legs of a right triangle is equal to the square of the length of the hypotenuse?

context.title: Pythagorean theorem
context.text: The Pythagorean theorem is a fundamental relation in Euclidean geometry among the three sides of a right triangle.

context.title: Right triangle
context.text: A right triangle is a triangle in which one angle is a right angle.

question: Which Spanish artist is the co-founder of the Cubist movement and painted 'Guernica'?

context.title: Pablo Picasso
context.text: Pablo Ruiz Picasso was a Spanish painter, sculptor, printmaker, ceramicist and theatre designer.

context.title: Guernica (Picasso)
context.text: Guernica is a large oil painting on canvas by Spanish artist Pablo Picasso completed in June 1937.

question: Which Italian scientist, known for inventing the radio, was born in the same city as the headquarters of the automobile manufacturer Lamborghini?

context.title: Guglielmo Marconi
context.text: Guglielmo Marconi was an Italian inventor and electrical engineer, known for his pioneering work on long-distance radio transmission.

context.title: Bologna
context.text: Bologna is the largest city of the Emilia-Romagna region in Northern Italy. Guglielmo Marconi was born here, and the area is also home to Lamborghini's headquarters in nearby Sant'Agata Bolognese.

question: Which physicist, who won the Nobel Prize in 1921, worked on the phenomenon where light hitting a material causes the emission of electrons?

context.title: Photoelectric effect
context.text: The photoelectric effect is the emission of electrons when electromagnetic radiation, such as light, hits a material.

context.title: Albert Einstein
context.text: Albert Einstein was awarded the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics for his services to theoretical physics, and especially for his discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect.
